name: Corned Beef Hash (Claiborne)
recipeInstructions:
From "The New York Times Cook Book," Craig Claiborne.
Harper and Row, 1990.
1. Place the corned beef in a kettle and add water to cover about 2 inches. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 1 hour. Cover closely and continue cooking for another 3 hours.
2. Meanwhile, place potatoes in a saucepan with cold water covering them. Add salt and cook until almost tender but firm, about 20-30 minutes. Do not overcook; they will be used to cube the potatoes later. Drain and let cool. Peel the potatoes and cut into very small dice (about 3 1/2 cups).
3. When corned beef is cooked, remove and let cool. Melt butter in a skillet and add onions and green pepper. Cook until wilted.
4. Trim away and discard any fat from the corned beef. Cut it into thin slices, then cube the slices (about 5 cups).
5. Combine cubed meat with diced potatoes and onion mixture in a mixing bowl. Add egg, egg yolk, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper. Mix thoroughly until compact.
6. Cover and refrigerate overnight to allow flavors to develop.
7. Shape the mixture into 8 portions of similar size (or flatten onto a nonstick skillet). Heat butter or oil for each patty. Cook each side for 2-3 minutes until browned.
8. Serve with a poached egg on top, if desired.
